summary: Open SysDolphin exact-match decomp PR touching four baselib files. The PR body claims 2 newly matched functions with clean isolated and full-ship regression verification. Bot report identifies new matches for AXDriver_8038E498 and fn_80393C14, plus improvements in particle .data, sislib functions, and HSD_CObjGetLeftVector.

key_files.role: Newly matched AXDriver_8038E498 by changing array iteration from a carried pointer variable to direct indexed access on AXDriver_804C45A0.
Small improvement to HSD_CObjGetLeftVector by removing a temporary result variable and testing HSD_CObjGetUpVector directly.
Adds packed MCC error/report string storage, switches fn_80392CD8 to offsets into lbl_8040A540, and scopes pool_data off around fn_80393C14, which the bot report marks newly matched.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push / #pragma pool_data off around fn_80393C14
text_corpus issue_comment: fn_80393C14 99.77% -> 100.00%, +1 byte; particle .data 1.47% -> 5.46%, +620 bytes

key_files.role: Improves multiple SisLib functions through stack-buffer size changes, initialization, declaration ordering, typed string_buffer indexing, and restoring calls to HSD_SisLib_803A8134.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 buffer and encoded[0] changes
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C declaration order and string_buffer access cleanup
text_corpus issue_comment: improvements in HSD_SisLib_803A84BC, HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C

what_changed.text: AXDriver_8038E498 now indexes AXDriver_804C45A0 directly inside the existing loop instead of using a local HSD_SM* cursor.
HSD_CObjGetLeftVector removes an intermediate res local and directly checks HSD_CObjGetUpVector(cobj, &up) == 0.
particle.c introduces static char lbl_8040A540[0x268] containing MCC error strings and report format strings; fn_80392CD8 now selects messages by offsets into that blob.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbl_8040A540 addition and fn_80392CD8 replacements

what_changed.text: particle.c wraps fn_80393C14 with #pragma push / #pragma pool_data off / #pragma pop.

sislib.c changes two local format buffers from 256 to 128 bytes, initializes encoded[0] in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, moves flag_hi declaration earlier in HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C, removes redundant casts around text->string_buffer access, and replaces two commented-out HSD_SisLib_803A8134 placeholders with real calls.

smart_moves.text: The AXDriver change keeps the natural for-loop and uses direct array indexing, avoiding a separate cursor variable while preserving the loop structure.
The SisLib string_buffer cleanup prefers the known field expression over repeated ((u8*) text->string_buffer) casts where byte indexing already expresses the access.
The particle pragma is tightly scoped with push/pop around only fn_80393C14 rather than left as a broad file-level setting.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push / #pragma pool_data off / #pragma pop around fn_80393C14

decomp_lessons.lesson: For array-like global storage, direct indexed access inside a natural loop can be a viable match shape compared with carrying and incrementing a local pointer cursor.
Removing an unnecessary local used only to hold a call result can improve matching when the source-level intent is simply a direct conditional call check.
When a field is already byte-addressable, using the field directly can be preferable to repeated raw u8* casts, while still using typed casts only at multi-byte reads.
Commented-out recovered calls can become matching source once the real arguments are known; here HSD_SisLib_803A8134 is restored with sis_cursor, text, and width/height output arguments.

naming_conventions.text: The PR preserves address-style names for uncertain symbols and functions such as fn_80392CD8, fn_80393C14, hsd_804CF7E8, and lbl_8040A540 rather than inventing semantic names.
Existing local role names such as eye, up, left, line_width_out, line_height_out, sis_cursor, and text are preserved where roles are already clear.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Array-indexing instead of pointer-cursor local for loop body codegen.
Direct conditional call expression instead of storing a temporary call result.
Packed string/data blob with explicit offsets for MCC error messages and OSReport format strings.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is data-section matching evidence in this PR, not a general recommendation to replace ordinary string literals with global blobs.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped #pragma pool_data off around a single function.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Global standards treat pragmas as exceptions requiring narrow scope and verifier evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Stack layout and local lifetime adjustments through buffer-size changes, explicit initialization, and declaration ordering.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: sislib.c buffer, encoded[0], and flag_hi changes

follow_up_queries: AXDriver_8038E498 AXDriver_804C45A0 indexed access pointer cursor
fn_80393C14 pragma pool_data off exact match
lbl_8040A540 MCC Error particle data blob

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: exact-match decompilation of 4 files, 2 newly matched functions, clean regression report
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report with new matches and improvements
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for changed files
changed_files.jsonl: four changed files with hunk counts
notes: No review comments or human reviewer feedback are present in the slice.
The PR is open and merged_at is null, so intake must not infer acceptance or merge status.
Verification claims are present in PR body and bot report; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.

The packed string blob in particle.c conflicts with general caution against string-literal-to-symbol regressions, but the PR evidence shows it was part of this slice's reported data improvement. Treat it as a PR-specific tactic for curator review, not a broad accepted standard.
The #pragma pool_data off tactic has narrow scope and reported matching benefit, but global standards require pragmas to remain exceptional.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Array indexing can beat pointer cursor locals for loop match shape
accepted_candidate_records.text: In AXDriver_8038E498, replacing a local HSD_SM* cursor and v++ with AXDriver_804C45A0[i] and &AXDriver_804C45A0[i] produced a reported 100% match while keeping the natural for-loop.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer known field access over redundant byte-pointer casts
accepted_candidate_records.text: HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C improved by changing repeated ((u8*) text->string_buffer)[pos] forms to text->string_buffer[pos], while retaining explicit typed casts for s16/u16/s32 reads.

source_update_candidates.title: Document tightly scoped pool_data pragma as exceptional matching tactic
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2659 reports fn_80393C14 reaching 100% after adding #pragma push / #pragma pool_data off / #pragma pop around only that function. This may be useful as an example under avoid-pragmas-register-asm or matching-tactics-need-evidence, emphasizing narrow scope and verifier evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential standards example only; current standards already warn pragmas are exceptions.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify handling of packed in-TU string blobs used for data-section matching
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2659 replaces inline MCC error/report strings with offsets into static char lbl_8040A540[0x268], and the bot report shows particle .data improvement. Curator should decide whether this is a legitimate data-ownership exception or a case to document as high-risk and PR-specific.
source_update_candidates.reason: The change intersects existing standards about literals/data ownership and no-string-literal-symbol regression; it should not be promoted without curator judgment.

source_update_candidates.title: SysDolphin baselib PR touched axdriver, cobj, particle, and sislib
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2659 is a SysDolphin baselib decomp-matching slice touching src/sysdolphin/baselib/axdriver.c, cobj.c, particle.c, and sislib.c, with reported new matches in AXDriver_8038E498 and fn_80393C14.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped historical matching context for future SysDolphin work.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that string literals should generally be converted into static data blobs; this PR only evidences a specific particle.c data/match tactic.
Do not infer reviewer approval or final project acceptance; the slice has no review comments and the PR is open.
Do not generalize #pragma pool_data off as normal style; evidence only supports a tightly scoped exception around fn_80393C14.
Do not claim all four files gained full function matches; the bot report names only AXDriver_8038E498 and fn_80393C14 as new matches, while other listed functions are improvements.
